status_notes: Initial research from Microsoft Defender for Cloud documentation, 2026-04-04. Pass 1 (service docs). Needs Pass 2 (API), Pass 3 (cross-reference), and Pass 5 (human review with live Azure environment). Note: brief summary also exists in monitor.json.
notes: Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) and Cloud Workload Protection (CWP). Free tier provides recommendations. Defender plans provide per-resource threat detection.

match_nodes.description: Cloud Security Posture Management — recommendations and Secure Score

data.description: Cloud Security Posture Management provides security recommendations based on Azure Security Benchmark. Free Foundational CSPM includes Secure Score, basic recommendations, and Azure Security Benchmark assessment. Defender CSPM (paid) adds attack path analysis, cloud security graph, agentless scanning, governance rules, and data-aware security posture.

options.description: Secure Score, security recommendations, Azure Security Benchmark assessment. Always available at no cost.
Attack path analysis, cloud security graph, agentless vulnerability scanning, agentless secret scanning, governance rules, data-aware security posture, external attack surface management.

default.note: Foundational CSPM is automatically available for all Azure subscriptions. Defender CSPM must be explicitly enabled.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Foundational CSPM for all subscriptions. Enable Defender CSPM for production environments to get attack path analysis and agentless scanning. Review Secure Score regularly and prioritize high-severity recommendations.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Microsoft Defender for Cloud → Overview → Secure Score

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name CloudPosture --tier Standard
configure.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → CSPM

data.description: Threat detection and advanced security for Azure VMs, on-premises servers, and multi-cloud (AWS, GCP) VMs. Two plans: Plan 1 (basic endpoint detection and response via Defender for Endpoint integration) and Plan 2 (adds vulnerability assessment, file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, adaptive application controls, adaptive network hardening).

options.description: Defender for Endpoint integration, endpoint detection and response (EDR), attack surface reduction.

Plan 1 plus: vulnerability assessment (Qualys or Microsoft Defender), file integrity monitoring, just-in-time VM access, adaptive application controls, adaptive network hardening, Docker host hardening.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable Plan 2 for production servers. Use just-in-time VM access to reduce attack surface. Review vulnerability assessment findings regularly. Configure file integrity monitoring for critical system files.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → Servers

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard --subplan P2

match_nodes.description: Threat detection for Azure Storage accounts

data.description: Threat detection for Azure Storage accounts (Blob, Files, Data Lake). Detects unusual access patterns, anonymous access from suspicious IPs, malicious file uploads, and sensitive data exposure. Uses activity monitoring (behavioral analysis) and Malware Scanning (hash reputation and on-upload scanning).

options.description: Behavioral analysis of storage access patterns. Detects anomalous access, data exfiltration, unusual anonymous access.
On-upload malware scanning for Blob Storage. Scans uploaded blobs using Microsoft Defender Antivirus engine.
Identifies exposure of sensitive data. Requires Defender CSPM with sensitive data discovery.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable for all storage accounts containing sensitive data. Enable Malware Scanning for storage accounts that accept user uploads.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → Storage

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name StorageAccounts --tier Standard

match_nodes.description: Threat detection for SQL databases and other database services

data.description: Threat detection for Azure SQL Database, SQL Managed Instance, SQL Server on VMs, open-source relational databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB), and Azure Cosmos DB. Detects SQL injection attempts, brute force attacks, anomalous access patterns, and suspicious database activities.

options.description: Threat detection for Azure SQL Database and SQL Managed Instance
Threat detection for SQL Server on Azure VMs, on-premises, and multi-cloud
Threat detection for Azure Database for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB
Threat detection for Azure Cosmos DB accounts

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable for all production databases. Review and remediate SQL vulnerability assessment findings. Configure alerts for SQL injection and brute force detections.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → Databases

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name SqlServers --tier Standard

match_nodes.description: Container image scanning, runtime threat detection, Kubernetes admission control

data.description: Security for containerized environments across AKS, Arc-enabled Kubernetes, and other supported Kubernetes distributions. Provides vulnerability assessment for container images (in ACR and runtime), runtime threat detection for Kubernetes clusters, Kubernetes admission control (Azure Policy for Kubernetes), and agentless discovery of Kubernetes resources.

options.description: Scan container images in ACR and running images for OS and language vulnerabilities. Powered by Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management.
Detect suspicious activity in Kubernetes clusters: privileged container creation, suspicious process execution, anomalous Kubernetes API calls.
Azure Policy for Kubernetes (Gatekeeper). Enforce security baselines on pod creation.
Inventory of Kubernetes resources without deploying agents.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable for all subscriptions with AKS clusters. Ensure the Defender profile/extension is deployed to clusters. Use admission control to prevent privileged containers and enforce image pull policies.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → Containers

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name Containers --tier Standard

match_nodes.description: Detects unusual Key Vault access patterns

data.description: Threat detection for Azure Key Vault. Detects unusual and potentially harmful attempts to access or exploit key vaults. Alerts on suspicious access patterns such as access from unusual locations, access from TOR exit nodes, unusual volume of operations, suspicious policy changes, and access from suspicious IP addresses.

default.note: Must be explicitly enabled as part of the Defender for Key Vault plan.

data.vendor_recommendation: Enable for all subscriptions with key vaults containing production secrets, keys, or certificates. Review alerts for unusual access patterns.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Defender plans → Key Vault

configure.cli: az security pricing create --name KeyVaults --tier Standard

data.description: Compliance dashboards that continuously assess Azure resources against regulatory standards and industry benchmarks. Maps Defender for Cloud recommendations to compliance controls. Built-in standards include Azure Security Benchmark (default), NIST SP 800-53, ISO 27001, PCI DSS, CIS Benchmarks, SOC 2, HIPAA, and more. Custom standards can be added.

options.description: Default. Microsoft's best-practice recommendations based on common compliance frameworks.
US government security controls framework
International information security management standard
Payment card industry data security standard
Center for Internet Security benchmark for Azure
Service organization controls for security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, privacy
Add custom compliance standards with your own controls

data.vendor_recommendation: Assign regulatory standards relevant to your industry. Use the compliance dashboard to track progress and prioritize remediation. Export compliance reports for auditors.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Regulatory compliance

configure.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → {subscription} → Security policies
configure.note: Regulatory compliance standards are assigned via security policy. Azure Security Benchmark is assigned by default.

match_nodes.description: Centralized alert management with severity classification

data.description: Centralized management for all security alerts generated by Defender for Cloud plans. Alerts are generated when threats are detected in protected resources. Severity levels: High, Medium, Low, and Informational. Alerts can be correlated into security incidents. Integration with Microsoft Sentinel for SIEM/SOAR capabilities. Supports suppression rules, alert automation (workflow automation via Logic Apps), and email notifications.

options.description: High confidence that a resource is compromised. Investigate immediately.
Suspicious activity that may indicate a compromise. Investigate promptly.
Benign positive or blocked attack. Review when possible.
Informational finding, no immediate action required.

data.vendor_recommendation: Configure email notifications for High severity alerts. Set up workflow automation for common alert types. Integrate with Microsoft Sentinel for advanced investigation and response. Review and tune suppression rules regularly.

audit.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Security alerts

configure.cli_notification: az security auto-provisioning-setting update --name default --auto-provision On
configure.console: Azure Portal → Defender for Cloud → Environment settings → Email notifications
configure.note: Alert generation is automatic for enabled Defender plans. Configuration focuses on notification routing and suppression rules.
